response: The internet traces its roots to 1960s U.S.-funded ARPANET research, first demonstrated in 1969, and was solidified in 1983 with the adoption of TCP/IP. Key milestones followed: Tim Berners-Lee’s creation of the World Wide Web in 1989, Mosaic’s 1993 browser, the late-1990s dot-com boom and 2001 crash (from which firms like Amazon and Google emerged), and the mid-2000s rise of social media platforms such as Facebook, Twitter, and YouTube. The 2007 iPhone popularized mobile internet, while 2006’s AWS launch signaled the dawn of large-scale cloud computing. Today the internet links more than 5 billion people and underpins nearly all facets of global commerce, communication, and entertainment.
